reasoning: 8x zoom reveals a diagonal pixel-art ice sword. Blade uses 3 hard tones (white highlight, light blue, mid blue) with no gradient abuse — clean. Outlines are mostly 1px dark, but the crossguard area shows 2px on one side and missing/1px on the other, a minor inconsistency. Handle pixels have slight left/right asymmetry around the pommel that would be surprising in hand-crafted art. Background shows scattered noise pixels that are likely PNG compression artifacts rather than sprite edge bleed, but cannot fully rule out sub-pixel overspray from generation. No melting details, no color bleed into adjacent regions, no semantic failure — sword reads clearly. The dual-view (4x left, 8x right) confirms the small version is consistent with a proper downscale. Not a clear fail, but guard inconsistency and handle asymmetry prevent a clean pass.
